description: Evaluates whether session work or durable knowledge should be logged before stopping

hooks.prompt:
Review the pending user prompt. If it does not contain durable, reusable knowledge, respond {"continue": true}.

Durable knowledge includes project decisions, requirements, explanations, processes, historical context, or answers that are likely to help in a future session. Skip greetings, transient status updates, coordination chatter, secrets, or one-off logistics.

If the prompt does contain durable knowledge, respond with {"continue": true, "hookSpecificOutput": {"additionalContext": "The latest user prompt appears to contain reusable knowledge. If you use it as authoritative context, an answer, a requirement, or a decision during this task, capture it before finishing with mcp__plugin_halyard_ask-expert__summarize_work using the most appropriate entry_type (DECISION, PROCESS, or CONTEXT), unless the same knowledge is already captured in this session. If prior work on this topic may already exist, also consider calling mcp__plugin_halyard_ask-expert__search_knowledge before planning or delegating."}}.
